mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to price in lower corporate earnings and driving negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE remains above 5.0 for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm robust consumer spending and credit availability, jointly driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series benchmarks, or 60-day forward returns turn negative when both series rise concurrently over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding the 3-month moving average during early recovery phases indicates rapid capacity utilization, triggering short-term momentum buying and positive weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative 5-day returns during early recovery phases, or Rank IC drops below zero when industrial production accelerates but equity markets remain flat.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth surpassing 2.5 percent annually forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally following CPIAUCSL breaches above 2.5 percent, or the negative predictive relationship vanishes when inflation spikes are accompanied by strong GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls sustains household income and aggregate demand, supporting corporate profit margins and delivering positive S&P 500 returns over quarterly horizons.
failure_condition: Rolling 60-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with payroll growth, or the signal reverses direction during periods of labor market tightness.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales reveals underlying economic resilience, where strong retail sales offset weak GDP prints to sustain positive monthly equity momentum.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression, or 20-day returns turn negative when retail sales outpace GDP growth over consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production combined with falling employment signal imminent economic contraction, triggering rapid risk-off positioning and negative weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in over 70 percent of triggered instances, or the joint decline fails to predict short-term drawdowns during structural supply shocks.
